content.problemStatement:
You have two arrays AAA and BBB, each of size NNN.

You can rearrange both of these arrays in any way you want.

After rearranging AAA and BBB, construct an array CCC of size NNN, where Ci=Ai+BiC_i = A_i + B_iCi​=Ai​+Bi​ for all 1≤i≤N1\le i \le N1≤i≤N.

Is it possible to have all elements in the array CCC equal?

If yes, print suitable rearrangements of AAA and BBB, else print −1-1−1 to mean no solution.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 111 integer NNN, the size of the arrays AAA and BBB.
The second line contains NNN integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the array AAA.
The third line contains NNN integers, B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, the array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, if there exists a suitable rearrangement of AAA and BBB, let DDD and EEE represent the respective rearranegments of AAA and BBB, then, print two lines :



The first line contains NNN space-separated integers D1,D2,…,DND_1, D_2, \ldots, D_ND1​,D2​,…,DN​. Note that this must be a rearrangement of AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers E1,E2,…,ENE_1, E_2, \ldots, E_NE1​,E2​,…,EN​. Note that this must be rearrangement of BBB.

If multiple solutions are possible, you may print any one solution.


If there doesn't exist a suitable rearrangement, just output a new line containing the integer −1-1−1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We rearrange array AAA as [3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2] and array BBB as [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2]. Then, the array CCC becomes [4,4,4][4, 4, 4][4,4,4].
Test case 222: We rearrange array AAA as [8,9][8, 9][8,9] and array BBB as [6,5][6, 5][6,5]. Then, the array CCC becomes [14,14][14, 14][14,14].
Test case 333: It can be shown that no rearrangements of AAA and BBB make all elements in CCC equal.
